title: Atomicinstall
body:
add install and uninstall commands to mimic the atomic cli commands so users
    can use podman in place of the atomic cli.
    
    the install command leverages an INSTALL label that has an embeddded command to
    run to prepare the OS or container for the system.
    
    the uninstall command leverges an UNINSTALL label that has an embedded command
    that cleans the system including deleting the image (and its containers).

comments.body: @rhatdan PTAL  I think some alignment on options, etc needs to occur.
LGTM
You need to update completions
Update commands.md
Is there any vision/plan for how commands that don't exist in docker are handled?  What would happen if upstream docker added a different `install`?
@cgwalters No plans for that.

> Is there any vision/plan for how commands that don't exist in docker are handled? What would happen if upstream docker added a different `install`?

I still think a generic approach would be better for anything other than `RUN`. My [proposal](https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/653#issuecomment-412063901) was to us `runl` top level command which means *runlabel*. Backwards compatibility with Atomic CLI is a concern, but what we could do for those cases is make it a config option so for example a config option like `atomic.cli.compat = True` and then

```
# podman install quay.io/foocontainer
atomic.cli.compat = True... Applying compat `podman install` -> `podman runl --label=install`
....
```

eventually remove the compat layer.
bot, retest this please

I still think we should first investigate a cleaner way to "install" containers instead of running arbitrary commands in the host and let every image reinvent a package manager.  What use cases do we want to cover with this feature?  Anything more than copying files to the host?

> I still think we should first investigate a cleaner way to "install" containers instead of running arbitrary commands in the host and let every image reinvent a package manager. 

:+1:

This is just to cover the existing atomic install use case.  We have been asked to have a solution by teams that are relying on this, to be used in systems that will no longer have Atomic.

@dustymabe Has suggested that we just have a podman containers runl --label "INSTALL,UNINSTALL,RUN" command that mimics the atomic install command, rather then have individual commands, Which  would be ok, except that atomic install and uninstall also removed added and removed the image.
does the solution need to be backwards compatible btw?

> This is just to cover the existing atomic install use case. We have been asked to have a solution by teams that are relying on this, to be used in systems that will no longer have Atomic.

I think what @giuseppe was saying is that the existing functionality is too open ended for us to fit into a nice pattern and support. I think supporting that open endedness (for backwards compatibility) is OK but knowing where we want to go and listing the "backwards compatibility" functionality as deprecated and "you need to use XYZ method in the future" would be best here. If we know that then we could put this backwards compatibility functionality under `podman atomic install` or `podman compat install` and spit out a deprecation warning. 

> 
> @dustymabe Has suggested that we just have a podman containers runl --label "INSTALL,UNINSTALL,RUN" command that mimics the atomic install command, rather then have individual commands, Which would be ok, except that atomic install and uninstall also removed added and removed the image.

I don't see any reason why `podman runl --label=INSTALL quay.io/img` couldn't download the image first. The uninstall behavior would change, yes.
Well for the runl version, I would prefer that it use containers/image / SKopeo method for looking at the remote image if not local and seeing if it has a INSTALL label before downloading.

> Well for the runl version, I would prefer that it use containers/image / SKopeo method for looking at the remote image if not local and seeing if it has a INSTALL label before downloading.

yeah that makes perfect sense to me
if we settle on runl, then imho we should just add --label to run and be done with it

<!-- test report -->

> if we settle on runl, then imho we should just add --label to run and be done with it

- is `--label` a valid `podman run` option today? could it ever be in the future?
- would we require that be the only option provided if we did it that way? i.e. `podman run --label=run quay.io/foo` would be valid but `podman run --label=run -v /path/to/vol:/path/to/vol quay.io/foo` would not be valid
`--label` exists already for adding labels
Yes runl or runlabel is a better idea then run. Since their is a very different CLI.
if the complaint is that container install|uninstall bloats and confuses, then I dont think we should be adding any subcommands and in particular one on the first level.

I don't want it at the primary level.
This would be 
podman containers runlabel
Ok, are you willing to sacrifice the container and image removal for uninstall?  And do you want me to implement this?
Lets have a conversation on this today.
Re-design is #1528
